title: Attio Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Attio** is a modern, customisable CRM (people, companies, deals, custom objects) exposing a REST API under `https://api.attio.com/v2`. This connector ‚Äî `AttioConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "attio"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) ‚Äî wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from an Attio workspace:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Workspaces | `/self` | Identify the workspace the token belongs to |
| Objects | `/objects` | List object schemas (people, companies, deals, custom) |
| Records | `/objects/{slug}/records` | List / get / create / update / assert / delete records |
| Attributes | `/objects/{slug}/attributes` | List / read attribute schemas |
| Lists | `/lists` | List Attio lists + their entries |
| Notes | `/notes` | List + create notes attached to records |
| Tasks | `/tasks` | List + create tasks |

The connector normalises records + notes + tasks into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC ‚Äî all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::AttioHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate an Attio access token

1. Sign in to https://app.attio.com.
2. Open **Workspace settings ‚Üí Developers ‚Üí Access tokens**.
3. Click **Create token**, give it a name + scopes (record:read, record:write, list:read, note:read, note:write, task:read, task:write as needed).
4. Copy the token value ‚Äî Attio shows it only once.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP ‚Üí Manage Connectors ‚Üí Attio**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_key` (secret) | Access token from step 4 |
| `workspace_slug` (text, optional) | Display label (e.g. `acme`) |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://api.attio.com/v2` (rarely needed) |
| `sync_objects` (text, optional) | Comma-separated object slugs to ingest, default `people,companies` |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 100 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `api_key` is present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `AttioHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which calls `GET /self` as a lightweight probe. On 2xx ‚Üí `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 ‚Üí `OFFLINE + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 ‚Üí `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 ‚Üí `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Attio REST API uses **server-to-server access token authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). The token is long-lived (until revoked), scoped to a single workspace, and sent in the `Authorization` header with the `Bearer` prefix.

### Credential model

- `api_key` is sent as `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>`.
- `workspace_slug` is display-only ‚Äî Attio derives the workspace from the token automatically.
- No token exchange, no refresh, no expiry (until revoked in the Attio UI).

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_key`. Initialises `AttioHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any Attio endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the configured api_key (no exchange). |
| `health_check()` | `GET /self` ‚Äî minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called ‚Äî no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Bearer <api_key>
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` ‚Äî never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `self.api_key` | `Authorization: Bearer <‚Ä¶>` value |
| `workspace_slug` | text | no | `self.workspace_slug` | Display-only label |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://api.attio.com/v2`) | Override for Attio sandbox or proxy |
| `sync_objects` | text | no | `self.sync_objects` (default `["people", "companies"]`) | Object slugs iterated by `sync()` |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 100) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_key"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
ATTIO_BASE_URL = "https://api.attio.com/v2"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `AttioConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::AttioHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_key`; initialise `AttioHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
Returns a passthrough TokenInfo with `access_token = self.api_key`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
`GET /self`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates configured `sync_objects` (default `people,companies`). Calls `POST /objects/{slug}/records/query`, normalises each record ‚Üí `NormalizedDocument` ‚Üí `ingest_batch`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_found / synced / failed`.

children.title: Objects + Attributes
children.content:
### `async list_workspaces() -> Dict`
`GET /self` wrapped in `{workspaces: [...]}`.

### `async list_objects() -> Dict`
`GET /objects` ‚Äî all object schemas.

### `async list_attributes(object_slug) -> Dict`
`GET /objects/{slug}/attributes`.

### `async get_attribute(object_slug, attribute_id) -> Dict`
`GET /objects/{slug}/attributes/{id}`.

children.title: Records (CRUD + Assert)
children.content:
### `async list_records(object_slug, limit=50, offset=0, filter=None, sorts=None) -> Dict`
`POST /objects/{slug}/records/query` ‚Äî body: `{limit, offset, filter?, sorts?}`.

### `async get_record(object_slug, record_id) -> Dict`
`GET /objects/{slug}/records/{record_id}`.

### `async create_record(object_slug, values) -> Dict`
`POST /objects/{slug}/records` ‚Äî body: `{data:{values:{‚Ä¶}}}`.

### `async update_record(object_slug, record_id, values) -> Dict`
`PATCH /objects/{slug}/records/{record_id}` ‚Äî body: `{data:{values:{‚Ä¶}}}`.

### `async assert_record(object_slug, matching_attribute, values) -> Dict`
`PUT /objects/{slug}/records?matching_attribute={attr}` ‚Äî upsert by matching attribute.

### `async delete_record(object_slug, record_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /objects/{slug}/records/{record_id}`.

children.title: Lists
children.content:
### `async list_lists() -> Dict`
`GET /lists`.

### `async get_list(list_id) -> Dict`
`GET /lists/{id}`.

### `async list_list_entries(list_id, limit=50, offset=0) -> Dict`
`POST /lists/{id}/entries/query`.

children.title: Notes + Tasks
children.content:
### `async list_notes(parent_object=None, parent_record_id=None, limit=50, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /notes` ‚Äî optionally scoped to a parent record.

### `async create_note(parent_object, parent_record_id, title, content, format="plaintext") -> Dict`
`POST /notes` ‚Äî body: `{data:{parent_object, parent_record_id, title, format, content}}`.

### `async list_tasks(limit=50, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /tasks`.

### `async create_task(content, format="plaintext", deadline_at=None, assignees=None, linked_records=None) -> Dict`
`POST /tasks`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
AttioError                            # base; carries status_code + response_body
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ AttioAuthError                    # 401 / 403
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ AttioBadRequestError              # 400
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ AttioNotFoundError                # 404
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ AttioConflictError                # 409
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ AttioRateLimitError               # 429 ‚Äî retry_after_s
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ AttioServerError                  # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
AttioConnectorError = AttioError
AttioAPIError       = AttioError
AttioNetworkError   = AttioServerError
AttioNotFound       = AttioNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `_BACKOFF_BASE * 2 ** attempt` (0.5s, 1s, 2s), retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `AttioServerError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 ‚Üí ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 ‚Üí ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 ‚Üí ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   ‚Äî logged as warning
other ‚Üí ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, FAILED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_key` blank | Re-enter the access token in ACP. Attio shows the token only once at creation ‚Äî rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from Attio ‚Äî token lacks scope | In Attio ‚Üí Workspace settings ‚Üí Developers ‚Üí Access tokens, edit the token and grant the missing scopes (record:read, record:write, etc.). |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 ‚Äî token revoked or never valid | Generate a new access token in Attio and re-install. |
| `attio.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Attio per-token rate cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or reduce sync frequency. |
| 404 on `get_record` | Wrong `object_slug` OR record deleted | Check the slug is one of the workspace's object slugs (`list_objects()`) and the record still exists. |
| `TypeError: Can't instantiate abstract class AttioConnector` | Older builds shipped without `sync()` ‚Äî abstract method missing | Fixed in v1.0.0 ‚Äî `sync()` iterates configured `sync_objects`. Re-pull the connector. |
| Sync returns 0 documents | `sync_objects` list empty | Set `sync_objects=people,companies` (or whatever slugs you want) in the install fields. |
